publisher.name: Phos Labs Commerce Intelligence (example authored by AI Village GPT-5.1)

protocols.context_architecture: Stateless HTTP / MCP-style skills that analyze and optimize commerce funnels, pricing, churn, and experiments. Longer-lived state (funnels, experiments, customer cohorts) typically lives in the caller's systems.
notes: This file is an illustrative example of /.well-known/lambda-atoms.json for Phos Labs Commerce Intelligence, authored by AI Village.
The atoms are designed so that external agents can treat responses from Phos Labs skills as structured events or states in their own continuity logs.

Nothing in this file is normative for Phos Labs unless they choose to adopt or adapt it. Field names in lambda_spec are pseudocode and should be aligned with the actual wire format used in a given integration.

atoms.description: Snapshot of a customer funnel with per-step conversion and identified behavioral barriers, typically returned by a diagnose-dropoff style skill.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'diagnose-dropoff' and isinstance(res.get('funnel', {}).get('steps'), list)
notes: Intended to capture a structured representation of a funnel, including step names, conversion rates, and friction points.
External agents may want to persist the full funnel object and track changes to it over time as separate Pl/funnel_dropoff states.

examples.label: Checkout funnel with sharp payment-page drop-off
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'diagnose-dropoff' and any(step.get('dropoff_rate', 0) > 0.4 for step in res.get('funnel', {}).get('steps', []))

atoms.description: Proposed change set to reduce friction in a checkout or signup flow, usually produced by a fix-checkout style skill.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'fix-checkout' and bool(res.get('recommended_changes'))
notes: Represents a transition between two versions of a funnel or flow: current vs. recommended.
External agents can connect Pl/checkout_fix to preceding Pl/funnel_dropoff states and to later performance metrics in their own logs.

examples.label: Add progress indicator and reduce form fields
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: 'add_progress_indicator' in res.get('recommended_changes', []) or any(c.get('type') == 'remove_field' for c in res.get('recommended_changes', []))

atoms.description: A pricing and packaging recommendation including anchors, tiers, and framing suggestions, often returned by an optimize-pricing style skill.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'optimize-pricing' and isinstance(res.get('pricing', {}).get('tiers'), list)
notes: Captures the behavioral framing around prices (e.g., decoy tiers, anchoring, charm pricing).
Multiple Pl/pricing_frame states over time can be used to study how pricing strategy evolves as product and market conditions change.

examples.label: Three-tier plan with decoy middle tier
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'optimize-pricing' and any(t.get('role') == 'decoy' for t in res.get('pricing', {}).get('tiers', []))

atoms.description: High predicted churn risk for a segment, cohort, or individual account, typically from a predict-churn style skill.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'predict-churn' and res.get('churn_risk', 0) >= 0.7
notes: Treats churn risk above a configurable threshold (here 0.7) as an alert-worthy event.
External agents can attach follow-up interventions (e.g., personalized outreach, offers) as separate atoms in their own domain.

examples.label: Enterprise segment with very high churn risk
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'predict-churn' and res.get('segment') == 'enterprise' and res.get('churn_risk', 0) >= 0.85

atoms.description: A structured experiment design, including arms, target metrics, minimum detectable effect, and planned duration, returned by a run-experiment style skill.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'run-experiment' and isinstance(res.get('experiment', {}).get('arms'), list)
notes: Provides a durable description of an experiment that other systems can reference when logging results.
External agents may want to promote the experiment ID from this state into their own metrics pipelines as a primary key.

examples.label: A/B test on checkout copy with conversion as primary metric
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('skill') == 'run-experiment' and res.get('experiment', {}).get('primary_metric') == 'checkout_conversion_rate'
